character|chr_0027_tangtang|skill|chr_00:
BASIC ATTACK: 
An attack with up to 5 sequences that deals <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</>. As the controlled operator, <#ba.lastcombo>Final Strike</> also deals <@ba.poise>18</> Stagger. 

DIVE ATTACK: 
Basic attack performed in mid-air becomes a dive attack that deals <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</> to nearby enemies. 

FINISHER: 
Basic attack performed near a Staggered enemy becomes a finisher that deals massive <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</> and recovers some SP.

Tangtang jumps out from the water and fires a point-blank shot that deals <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</> to the enemy and creates a <@ba.key>Waterspout</>.
Casting the battle skill also consumes all nearby <@ba.key>Whirlpools</> to create additional <@ba.key>Waterspouts</> and <#ba.return>return</> some SP based on the number of Waterspouts created.
<@ba.key>Waterspout</>: Applies 1 stack of <#ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> on enemies within range and deals <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</> over time. If the battle skill creates multiple <@ba.key>Waterspouts</>, they also apply <#ba.spellvul>Arts Susceptibility</> to enemies (but not another stack of <#ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</>).
<@ba.info>DMG dealt by the Waterspout is treated as Battle Skill DMG.</>

Tangtang opens her eyepatch and unleashes an <@ba.key>OLDEN STARE</> from her eye that temporarily locks down, immobilizes, and deals <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</> over time to enemies within its area of effect.
When the <@ba.key>OLDEN STARE</> stops evolving, a rogue wave comes crashing down to deal massive <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</> to enemies in its area of effect.
If the controlled operator performs a dive attack in the <@ba.key>OLDEN STARE</> pattern, its evolution ends and the rogue wave is summoned immediately to deal even more <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</>.

COMBO TRIGGER: When applying <#ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> or dealing <#ba.spellburst>Arts Burst DMG</> to an enemy.
Tangtang unleashes a powerful jet stream that penetrates all enemies in its path, deals <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</> to them, and creates a <@ba.key>Whirlpool</>.
<@ba.info>Up to 2 Whirlpools can exist in the battlefield at any time.</>
character|chr_0027_tangtang|talent|chr_0: When within a <@ba.vup>5</>-meter radius of a <@ba.key>Whirlpool</>, allied operators gain <@ba.vup>10%</> <#ba.speedup>Haste</> while enemies suffer <@ba.vup>20%</> <#ba.slow>Slow</>. These effects persist for 3s after leaving the area of effect.

<@ba.key>Waterspouts</> created this way have the same effects as those created by the battle skill <@ba.key>IMA WAVERIDAAH!</> and gain DMG Dealt <@ba.vup>+40%</>.

<@ba.key>MOVE 1 - Cloudtrapper</>: Costs <@ba.vup>100</> SP and <#ba.return>returns</><@ba.vup> 50</> SP after casting; Mi Fu fires a grappling chain from her gauntlets that deals <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> to the target and nearby enemies, then attempts to pull the target and nearby enemies towards her. After casting MOVE 1, the next battle skill will be temporarily replaced with <@ba.key>Trail and Mangle</>.

<@ba.key>MOVE 3 - World Splitter</>: Costs <@ba.vup>50</> SP; Mi Fu deals massive <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> to a specified area; this DMG is not treated as Battle Skill DMG, but as <#ba.crush>Crush</> DMG.

When the wearer applies <#ba.crush>Crush</> or <#ba.fracture>Breach</>, the wearer gains <@ba.pd>Physical DMG Dealt</> <@ba.vup>+[6% × Max number of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stacks <#ba.consume>consumed</> from one enemy]</> for 20s. If the target already has <#ba.physicalvul>Physical Susceptibility</>, is <@ba.poise>Staggered</>, or has attached <#ba.originium>Originium Crystals</>, increase the aforementioned buff to <@ba.vup>1.5</> times. This buff cannot stack.
equipment|item_equip_t4_suit_criti01_edc:
3-piece set effect: Wearer's Critical Rate <@ba.vup>+5%</>. 
After the wearer scores a critical hit, the wearer gains ATK <@ba.vup>+5%</> for 5s. This effect can reach 5 stacks. At max stacks, grant an additional Critical Rate <@ba.vup>+5%</>. This effect cannot stack.
equipment|item_equip_t4_suit_criti01_han:
3-piece set effect: Wearer's Critical Rate <@ba.vup>+5%</>. 
After the wearer scores a critical hit, the wearer gains ATK <@ba.vup>+5%</> for 5s. This effect can reach 5 stacks. At max stacks, grant an additional Critical Rate <@ba.vup>+5%</>. This effect cannot stack.

3-piece set effect: Wearer's Critical Rate <@ba.vup>+5%</>. 
After the wearer scores a critical hit, the wearer gains ATK <@ba.vup>+5%</> for 5s. This effect can reach 5 stacks. At max stacks, grant an additional Critical Rate <@ba.vup>+5%</>. This effect cannot stack.
equipment|item_equip_t4_suit_fire_natr01: Hot Work

3-piece set effect: Wearer's Arts Intensity <@ba.vup>+30</>. 
After the wearer applies <#ba.burning>Combustion</>, the wearer gains <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> +<@ba.vup>50%</> for 10s. After the wearer applies <#ba.corrupt>Corrosion</>, the wearer gains <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</> +<@ba.vup>50%</> for 10s. The aforementioned effects cannot stack.
equipment|item_equip_t4_suit_expend_spel:
3-piece set effect: Wearer's ATK <@ba.vup>+10%</>.
Whenever the wearer <#ba.consume>consumes</> <#ba.conduct>Electrification</> or <#ba.corrupt>Corrosion</>, the wearer gains a number of buff stacks equal to the <#ba.statuslevel>Status Level</> of the Arts Reaction consumed, with each buff stack giving <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG Dealt</> and <@ba.natur>Nature DMG Dealt</> <@ba.vup>+15%</> for 25s. Max number of buff stacks: 3. Duration of each stack is counted separately.
equipment|item_equip_t4_suit_pulse_cryst:
3-piece set effect: Wearer's Arts Intensity <@ba.vup>+30</>. 
After the wearer applies <#ba.conduct>Electrification</>, the wearer gains <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</><@ba.vup> +50%</> for 10s. After the wearer applies <#ba.frozen>Solidification</>, the wearer gains <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</><@ba.vup> +50%</> for 10s. The aforementioned effects cannot stack.
equipment|item_equip_t4_suit_heal01_body:
3-piece set effect: Wearer's HP Treatment Efficiency <@ba.vup>+20%</>. 
After the wearer gives HP treatment to an allied target, that target also gains <@ba.vup>15%</> DMG Reduction against all types of DMG for 10s. If the said treatment exceeds the target's Max HP, the target gains <@ba.vup>30%</> DMG Reduction against all types of DMG. The aforementioned effects cannot stack.

After the wearer applies a <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</>, the wearer also performs 1 hit that deals 250% ATK of Physical DMG and [<@ba.poise>10</> Stagger]. Effect trigger cooldown: 15s.

After the wearer applies a <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</>, the wearer also performs 1 hit that deals 250% ATK of Physical DMG and [<@ba.poise>10</> Stagger]. Effect trigger cooldown: 15s.

After the wearer applies a <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</>, the wearer also performs 1 hit that deals 250% ATK of Physical DMG and [<@ba.poise>10</> Stagger]. Effect trigger cooldown: 15s.

glossary|adv%2EelectricFence|description: An active high voltage fence is impassable. Follow the cable, find the switch, and shut down the fence for safe passage.
glossary|adv%2Eeswitch|description: A reactive device changes its operation mode when attacked or hit by an explosion. It is often used to switch off <#adv.electricFence>High Voltage Fences</> or activate <#adv.jumpMachine>Bounce Devices</>.
glossary|sys%2EmanualCraftDish|descripti:
Fine Food & Meds (Fine F&M) can be used as <#sys.tacticalItem>tacticals</>. 
Unlike <#sys.manualCraftTonic>Emergency Food & Meds</>, they can only be made using <#sys.manualCraft>Crafting</>.
glossary|adv%2EjumpMachine|description:
The controlled operator can use Bounce Devices to jump across a longer distance or reach high places unreachable with normal jumping.
Simply stand on a powered Bounce Device, then jump to use it.
glossary|ba%2Efireburst|description: <#ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> + <#ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> will trigger <@ba.fire>Heat Burst</> that deals <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> to enemies.
glossary|adv%2EpowerTerminal|description: An electric nexus is usually placed at the end of a power transmission line. Connect it to the power grid to supply power to nearby facilities. Electric Nexuses are often used to power gates and switches. Supply power to Electric Nexuses with Relay Towers. This then grants control access to gates or switches linked to them.
glossary|sys%2EmanualCraftField|descript: <#sys.manualCraft>Crafting</> lets you craft planting plots with plant seeds and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</>. Planting plots can be placed and grown at the Eco-Farm.
glossary|ba%2Eabsorb|description: A way to end a Physical or Arts debuff early. <@ba.key>Absorb</> is not the same as <#ba.consume>consume</>.
glossary|ba%2Ecombo|description: Next battle skill or ultimate cast by the team consumes the Link buff and deals more DMG, with battle skills getting a higher increase. Effects of the same type can stack, but with diminishing returns.
glossary|item%2EportSoilBbflower|descrip:
Aketine Plots can be cultivated at the Eco-Farm to acquire <#item.bbflower>Aketine</>.
These plots can be crafted by using <#item.bbflowerSeed>Aketine Seed</> and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> in the Planting screen.
Harvesting a fully grown plot provides plenty of Aketine.
glossary|ba%2Eairborne|description: Applies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> on an enemy. If the enemy is already <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</>, then add 1 stack of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</>, and deal <@ba.phy>Physical DMG</> and <@ba.poise>Stagger</>.
glossary|ba%2Eburning|description:
An enemy with <@ba.burning>Combustion</> takes <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> over time.
<@ba.burning>Combustion</> = A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Heat Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</>
glossary|item%2EplantMossSpc1|descriptio:
The Firebuckle is a plant that grows in the wilderness and is known for its exceptionally brilliant flowers.
There is a chance of getting Firebuckles when harvesting <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflowers</> in the wilderness.
They can be used for crafting <#sys.tacticalItem>tacticals</>.
glossary|ba%2Eburningonchar|description:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.burning>Combusted</>: Temporarily take continuous <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> that ignores their DEF.
glossary|ba%2Econduct|description:
An enemy with <@ba.conduct>Electrification</> takes more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</>.
<@ba.conduct>Electrification</> = A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Electric Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</>
glossary|item%2EquartzPowder|description:
A powder produced by shredding <#item.quartzGlass>Amethyst Fiber</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
glossary|ba%2Econductonchar|description:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.conduct>Electrified</>: Temporary stun and take more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</> for a certain duration.
glossary|ba%2Enaturalenhance|description: Nature Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|ba%2Econsume|description: Various Physical and Arts debuffs can be ended early. For example, <#ba.crush>Crush</> consumes <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> debuffs, while <#ba.burning>Combustion</> consumes <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</> debuffs.
glossary|ba%2Ecorrupt|description:
An enemy with <@ba.corrupt>Corrosion</> gradually loses resistance against all elements. 
<@ba.corrupt>Corrosion</> = A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Nature Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</>
glossary|ba%2Ecorruptonchar|description:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.corrupt>Corroded</>: Cannot dash and suffer movement speed reduction for a certain duration.
glossary|ba%2Elastcombo|description: Final Strike is the last sequence of basic attack. When the controlled operator's Final Strike hits the enemy, it also deals <@ba.poise>Stagger</> and restores some Skill Points (SP).
glossary|ba%2Ecrush|description: Applies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> on an enemy. If the enemy is already <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</>, then <#ba.consume>consume</> all <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stacks and deal massive <@ba.phy>Physical DMG</>.
glossary|ba%2Ecrystbreak|description: To an enemy with active <#ba.frozen>Solidification</>, applying <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> or <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> effects will <#ba.consume>consume</> the <#ba.frozen>Solidification</> to trigger Shatter that deals massive <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>.
glossary|ba%2Enaturalvul|description: Nature Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|item%2Eoriginium|description:
An ore that can be gathered from Originium Ore mining spots in the wilderness. It can be used for power generation in the AIC Factory.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be refined to produce <#item.crystalShell>Origocrust</>.
glossary|ba%2Ecrystburst|description: <#ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> + <#ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> will trigger <@ba.cryst>Cryo Burst</> that deals <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</> to enemies.
glossary|ba%2Espellinflict|description:
<@ba.key>Arts Inflictions</> are special Arts debuff inflicted upon the enemy. There are 4 elements of Arts Inflictions: <#ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</>, <#ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</>, <#ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</>, and <#ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.key>Arts Infliction</> of the same element can be applied.
glossary|ba%2Ecrystenhance|description: Cryo Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|ba%2Ecrystinflict|description:
<@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> is a type of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> can be applied and stacking triggers <#ba.crystburst>Cryo Burst</>. 
A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Cryo Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> = <#ba.frozen>Solidification</>
glossary|item%2EplantMossSeed3|descripti:
Seeds picked from <#item.plantMoss3>Sandleaves</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Sandleaf Seeds can then be planted and grown at AIC Factory facilities to acquire Sandleaves.
Alternatively, Sandleaf Seed and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> can be used in the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen and crafted into <#item.portSoilMoss3>Sandleaf Plots</>.
glossary|ba%2Ecrystonchar|description:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.frozen>Solidified</>: Cannot move for a certain duration.
glossary|ba%2Efrozen|description:
An enemy with <@ba.frozen>Solidification</> cannot move, and applying <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> or a <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> to the enemy triggers the <#ba.crystbreak>Shatter</> effect. 
<@ba.frozen>Solidification</> = A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Cryo Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</>
glossary|ba%2Ecrystvul|description: Cryo Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|sys%2EmanualCraftTonic|descript:
Emergency Food & Meds (EMS F&M) can be used as <#sys.tacticalItem>tacticals</>.
They can be acquired by <#sys.manualCraft>Crafting</> or by using AIC Factory facilities.
glossary|ba%2Edispel|description: This removes specific debuffs from the operator.
glossary|ba%2Efirevul|description: Heat Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|ba%2Espeedup|description: Temporarily raises the movement speed of a target. The number represents the degree of the speed increase. Effects of the same type can stack until hitting the speed cap.
glossary|ba%2Edot|description: DMG over time cannot trigger critical hits.
glossary|ba%2Enaturalonchar|description:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.corrupt>Corroded</>: Cannot dash and suffer movement speed reduction for a certain duration.
glossary|ba%2Eslow|description: Slowed targets temporarily suffer movement speed reduction. The number represents the degree of reduction. When there are multiple effects of the same type, only the most powerful applies.
glossary|ba%2Eenhance|description: Amped targets deal more DMG of the element it is Amped for. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|ba%2Efireenhance|description: Heat Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|ba%2Epulseburst|description: <#ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> + <#ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> will trigger <@ba.pulse>Electric Burst</> that deals <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</> to enemies.
glossary|item%2EglassCmpt|description:
A part produced by fitting <#item.quartzGlass>Amethyst Fiber</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
glossary|ba%2Efireinflict|description:
<@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> is a type of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> can be applied and stacking triggers <#ba.fireburst>Heat Burst</>. 
A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Heat Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> = <#ba.burning>Combustion</>
glossary|ba%2Ereturn|description:
After spending SP and meeting certain criteria, some of the SP spent will be returned. 
SP Return is not "SP Recovery" and does not activate SP Recovery-triggered effects. Spending returned SP also does not give Ultimate Energy.
glossary|ba%2Eweak|description: Weakened targets temporarily deal reduced DMG. The number represents the degree of reduction. Effects of the same type can stack non-linearly.
glossary|ba%2Efireonchar|description:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.burning>Combusted</>: Temporarily take continuous <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> that ignores their DEF.
glossary|ba%2Efracture|description: Applies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> on an enemy. If the enemy is already <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</>, then <#ba.consume>consume</> all <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stacks, deal <@ba.phy>Physical DMG</>, and temporarily increase the <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> taken by the enemy.
glossary|item%2EplantSp1|description:
The Reed Rye is a type of plantable crop gatherable from the wilderness. It can also be acquired by cultivating <#item.portSoilSp1>Reed Rye Plots</>.
It can be directly used for crafting <#sys.manualCraftDish>Fine Food & Meds</> (Fine F&M).
glossary|ba%2Efrozenonchar|description:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.frozen>Solidified</>: Cannot move for a certain duration.
glossary|item%2EbottledRecHp1|descriptio:
A capsule made by <#sys.manualCraft>crafting</> or filling <#item.plantMossPowder1>Buckflower Powder</> into an <#item.glassBottle>Amethyst Bottle</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Use this to restore an operator's HP. It can also be equipped as a <#sys.tacticalItem>tactical</>.
glossary|item%2EquartzSand|description:
An ore that can be gathered from Amethyst mining spots in the wilderness.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be refined to produce <#item.quartzGlass>Amethyst Fiber</>.
glossary|ba%2Eguard|description: Protected targets temporarily take reduced DMG. The number represents the degree of reduction. When there are multiple effects of the same type, only the most powerful applies.
glossary|ba%2Eknockdown|description: Applies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> on an enemy. If the enemy is already <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</>, then add 1 stack of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</>, and deal <@ba.phy>Physical DMG</> and <@ba.poise>Stagger</>.
glossary|ba%2Enaturalburst|description: <#ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> + <#ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> will trigger <@ba.natur>Nature Burst</> that deals <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</> to enemies.
glossary|ba%2Enaturalinflict|description:
<@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> is a type of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> can be applied and stacking triggers <#ba.naturalburst>Nature Burst</>. 
A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Nature Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> = <#ba.corrupt>Corrosion</>
glossary|ba%2Enoguard|description:
Hitting an enemy for the first time with a skill with <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> applies <@ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> instead of the actual Physical Status.
A <@ba.phy>Vulnerable</> enemy can be <#ba.airborne>Lifted</> and <#ba.knockdown>Knocked Down</> to add more Vulnerability stacks (up to 4). Hitting a Vulnerable enemy with <#ba.crush>Crush</> or <#ba.fracture>Breach</> will <#ba.consume>consume</> the Vulnerability stacks.
glossary|item%2EoriginiumPowder|descript:
A powder produced by shredding <#item.originium>Originium Ore</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
glossary|ba%2Eoriginium|description: The Endministrator's combo skill attaches Originium Crystals to enemies and immobilizes them.
glossary|ba%2Ephysicalenhance|descriptio: Physical Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|item%2EironPowder|description: A powder produced by shredding <#item.ironNugget>Ferrium</> with AIC Factory facilities. It can be used to produce other materials.
glossary|item%2EprocBomb|description:
Industrial Explosives are a type of explosive that can be produced.
They are made by using the Packaging Unit, an AIC Factory facility. The materials required are <#item.glassCmpt>Amethyst Part</> and <#item.bbflowerPowder>Aketine Powder</>.
Use Industrial Explosives to deal DMG to enemies or take down destructible objects within the blast radius.
glossary|ba%2Ephysicalstatus|description: Physical Statuses refer to physical debuffs applied to the enemy. They include: <#ba.airborne>Lifted</>, <#ba.knockdown>Knocked Down</>, <#ba.crush>Crushed</>, and <#ba.fracture>Breached</>.
glossary|ba%2Epoiseknot|description: Some powerful enemies have <@ba.poise>Stagger Nodes</>. Deal enough <@ba.poise>Stagger</> to reach the <@ba.poise>Stagger Node</> to interrupt such enemies and make them stumble.
glossary|item%2EplantMossPowder3|descrip:
A powder produced by shredding <#item.plantMoss3>Sandleaves</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials, usually by grinding.
glossary|ba%2Ephysicalvul|description: Physical Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|ba%2Epulseenhance|description: Electric Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|ba%2Epulseinflict|description:
<@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> is a type of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> can be applied and stacking triggers <#ba.pulseburst>Electric Burst</>. 
A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Electric Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> = <#ba.conduct>Electrification</>
glossary|ba%2Epulseonchar|description:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.conduct>Electrified</>: Temporary stun and take more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</> for a certain duration.
glossary|ba%2Espelldmg|description:
Damage can be divided into <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>, <@ba.key>Arts DMG</>, and <@ba.ether>Æther DMG</>.
<@ba.key>Arts DMG</> can be further divided into <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</>, <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</>, <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</>, and <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</>.
glossary|ba%2Epulsevul|description: Electric Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|ba%2Erossi|description: Rossi's battle skill applies a special status on the enemy. The affected target will take <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> over time, and suffers higher Physical DMG Taken and Heat DMG Taken.
glossary|item%2EplantMoss1|description:
The Buckflower is a plant that grows in the wilderness and is known for its brightly colored flowers.
The plant can also be grown with AIC Factory facilities or <#item.portSoilMoss1>Buckflower Plots</>.
The plant can be used for crafting <#sys.tacticalItem>tacticals</> or other materials.
glossary|ba%2Eshield|description: The shield can absorb damage while it is active. Some shields may only absorb specific type(s) of DMG.
glossary|ba%2Espellburst|description: <@ba.key>Arts Bursts</> are triggered by stacking <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Inflictions</> of the same elements. They include <#ba.fireburst>Heat Burst</>, <#ba.pulseburst>Electric Burst</>, <#ba.crystburst>Cryo Burst</>, and <#ba.naturalburst>Nature Burst</>.
glossary|ba%2Espellenhance|description: Arts Amped targets temporarily deal more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|ba%2Espellinflictonchar|name: Arts Inflictions / Arts Reactions on Operators
glossary|ba%2Espellinflictonchar|descrip:
Some enemy skills can apply stacks of <#ba.burningonchar>Heat Infliction</>, <#ba.conductonchar>Electric Infliction</>, <#ba.frozenonchar>Cryo Infliction</>, or <#ba.corruptonchar>Nature Infliction</>.
At 4 stacks of the Arts Inflictions, the operator becomes <#ba.burningonchar>Combusted</>, <#ba.conductonchar>Electrified</>, <#ba.frozenonchar>Solidified</>, or <#ba.corruptonchar>Corroded</> respectively.
glossary|item%2EportSoilMoss3|descriptio:
Sandleaf Plots can be cultivated at the Eco-Farm to acquire <#item.plantMoss3>Sandleaf</>.
These plots can be crafted by using <#item.plantMossSeed3>Sandleaf Seed</> and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> in the Planting screen.
Harvesting a fully grown plot provides plenty of Sandleaves.
glossary|ba%2Espellstatus|description: <@ba.key>Arts Reactions</> are Arts debuffs that can be applied to the enemy. They include: <#ba.burning>Combustion</>, <#ba.conduct>Electrification</>, <#ba.frozen>Solidification</>, and <#ba.corrupt>Corrosion</>.
glossary|ba%2Espellvul|description: Arts Susceptible targets temporarily take more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|ba%2Estatuslevel|description: Status Level is the number of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> or <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</> stacks (max 4) <#ba.consume>consumed</> when triggering a <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> or <#ba.spellstatus>Arts Reaction</>. The higher the <#ba.statuslevel>Status Level</>, the stronger the resulting <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> or <#ba.spellstatus>Arts Reaction</>.
glossary|item%2EoriginiumEnrPowder|descr:
A fine, dense powder produced by grinding <#item.originiumPowder>Originium Powder</> and <#item.plantMossPowder3>Sandleaf Powder</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
glossary|ba%2Evulnerable|description: Susceptible targets temporarily take more DMG of the element it is Susceptible to. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
glossary|item%2EcarbonMtl|description:
A material produced by refining <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflowers</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Carbon is essential for crafting <#sys.manualCraftField>planting</> plots, and can also be used for producing other materials.
glossary|fac%2Ehub|description: The Protocol Automation-Core (PAC) is the central facility of the Automated Industry Complex (AIC) Factory. Storage management becomes available when standing in the Core AIC Area of the PAC.
glossary|item%2Ebbflower|description:
A plant that contains flammable and explosive substances. It can be gathered from the wilderness.
The plant can be grown with AIC Factory facilities or cultivated with <#item.portSoilBbflower>Aketine Plots</>.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be shredded to produce <#item.bbflowerPowder>Aketine Powder</>.
glossary|sys%2EmanualCraft|description:
Open the Backpack panel to access the Crafting screen. Different raw materials can be crafted into useful items.
For example, <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflower</> can be crafted into <#item.plantMossSeed1>Buckflower Powder</> for restoring HP.
glossary|item%2EbbflowerPowder|descripti:
A powder produced by shredding <#item.bbflower>Aketine</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
glossary|item%2EironEnrPowder|descriptio:
A fine, dense powder produced by grinding <#item.ironPowder>Ferrium Powder</> and <#item.plantMossPowder3>Sandleaf Powder</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
glossary|item%2EbbflowerSeed|description:
Seeds picked from <#item.bbflower>Aketine</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Aketine Seeds can then be planted and grown at AIC Factory facilities to acquire Aketine.
Alternatively, Aketine Seed and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> can be used in the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen and crafted into <#item.portSoilBbflower>Aketine Plots</>.
glossary|item%2EcrystalShell|description:
A crystalline crust produced by refining <#item.originium>Originium Ore</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
glossary|item%2EglassBottle|description:
A bottle produced by moulding <#item.quartzGlass>Amethyst Fiber</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
glossary|item%2EironNugget|description: A metal ingot produced by refining <#item.ironOre>Ferrium Ore</> with AIC Factory facilities. It can be used to produce other materials.
glossary|item%2EplantMoss3|description:
A plant that is characterized by rough, gritty leaves. It can be gathered from the wilderness.
The plant can be grown with AIC Factory facilities or cultivated with <#item.portSoilMoss3>Sandleaf Plots</>.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be shredded to produce <#item.plantMossPowder3>Sandleaf Powder</>.
glossary|item%2EironOre|description:
An ore that can be gathered from Ferrium Ore mining spots in the wilderness.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be refined to produce <#item.ironNugget>Ferrium</>.
glossary|item%2EplantMossPowder1|descrip:
A powder made by <#sys.manualCraft>crafting</> or shredding <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflowers</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Use this to restore an operator's HP. It can also be equipped as a <#sys.tacticalItem>tactical</>.
glossary|item%2EplantMossSeed1|descripti:
Seeds picked from <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflowers</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Buckflower Seeds can then be planted and grown at AIC Factory facilities to acquire Buckflowers. Alternatively, Buckflower Seed and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> can be used in the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen and crafted into <#item.portSoilMoss1>Buckflower Plots</>.
glossary|item%2EplantSpSeed1|description:
Seeds produced by seed-picking <#item.plantSp1>Reed Rye</> plants with AIC Factory facilities.
Take Reed Rye Seed and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</>, then go to the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen to craft them into <#item.portSoilSp1>Reed Rye Plots</>.
glossary|item%2EportSoilMoss1|descriptio:
Buckflower Plots can be cultivated at the Eco-Farm to acquire <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflower</>.
These plots can be crafted by using <#item.plantMossSeed1>Buckflower Seed</> and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> in the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen.
Harvesting a fully grown plot has a higher chance of getting bonus <#item.plantMossSpc1>Firebuckle</>.
glossary|item%2EportSoilSp1|description:
Reed Rye Plots can be cultivated at the Eco-Farm to acquire <#item.plantSp1>Reed Rye</>.
These plots can be crafted by using <#item.plantSpSeed1>Reed Rye Seed</> and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> in the Planting screen.
Harvesting a fully grown plot provides plenty of Reed Rye.
glossary|item%2EprocBattery1|description:
A battery produced by packaging <#item.glassCmpt>Amethyst Part</> and <#item.originiumPowder>Originium Powder</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to supply power, charge up facilities, or traded at various outposts.
glossary|item%2EquartzGlass|description:
A fiber produced by refining <#item.quartzSand>Amethyst Ore</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
glossary|sys%2EtacticalItem|description:
Tacticals are items that can be used during combat to provide various benefits and buffs. Every tactical has different activation criteria and usage limits.
Every operator can be equipped with 1 tactical. During battle, whenever the tactical activation criteria are met, the operator will auto-use their equipped tactical.
After expending all tactical usage attempts in a single battle, one more use will be restored after a while.
